threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘London Fields’ is just a park and not the name of a small empire of overpriced coffee shops.
Caught the N137 last night. A guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. You could feel the eye rolls and smiles at the same time.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I heard a kid argue with their mom about whether the ice cream truck's jingle counted as pop music.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a street art gallery under the U-Bahn tracks, complete with impromptu dance-offs and pop-up cafes. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows not to panic when the BART's down; they check the bus app instead, even when it says 'delayed' yet again.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo get off the Muni, check his watch, and sprint into the courthouse. Only in SF do you dress fancy and still ride the bus.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the placa, but most locals know the best spots for brunch are tucked away in those side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell when someone’s new; they’re the ones mistaking bocadillos for sandwich rolls at the local bar.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'thank you' to bus drivers. Just wait, you'll stop once you see the rolling eye of a local.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with classic soul. Everyone joined in for a verse; who knew Brixton had such talent at midnight?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a group of kids arguing over who could hold the biggest slice of döner while an old lady fed pigeons. That's Neukölln life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain does the sound of techno spill from a hidden bunker on a Sunday morning, while families picnic in nearby parks. It's a surreal combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to avoid Civic Center during BART meltdowns—especially when there's a dog on the tracks. Tourists just stand confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy casually juggling while waiting for the bus at 6th and Market. Only in SF do you get street performers and transit delays as your daily norm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the bohemian vibe, but it’s the locals queuing at the bakeries that keep the real heartbeat alive.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; they’re the ones still trying to navigate the metro with that old paper map, while everyone else swipes their T10 pass.
